findings.title: Empty-vault inflation attack can steal later deposits via zero-share minting

best_agent_match.title: Empty-vault inflation attack can steal later deposits via zero-share minting

findings.title: Permissionless repeated `earn()` calls can drain the withdrawal buffer to dust

best_agent_match.title: Permissionless repeated `earn()` calls can drain the vault's cash buffer to near zero

findings.title: `getPricePerFullShare()` reverts while the vault is empty

best_agent_match.title: `getPricePerFullShare()` reverts while the vault is empty

rejected_candidates.title: Controller set to address(0) permanently locks all funds
rejected_candidates.reason: Setting an invalid controller is a governance-only misconfiguration, and governance can later set a valid controller again; the claim of permanent lock is overstated and not a standalone protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Controller can drain any ERC20 tokens from vault via harvest()
rejected_candidates.reason: `harvest()` is an explicitly privileged controller-only sweep for non-underlying tokens; this is a trust/roles assumption, not an unprivileged exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero in withdraw when totalSupply is zero
rejected_candidates.reason: A meaningful withdraw cannot occur when `totalSupply()` is zero because no user holds shares then; this is only a trivial edge-case revert such as `withdraw(0)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero in deposit when _pool is zero
rejected_candidates.reason: The stated path is incorrect because if all users fully withdraw then `totalSupply()` also becomes zero, taking the first-deposit branch. The remaining `_pool == 0 && totalSupply() > 0` case requires prior total asset loss/insolvency, not a new logic flaw in `deposit()`.

rejected_candidates.title: setMin can be set to 0, disabling yield generation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a governance-controlled configuration choice rather than an exploitable vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection allows sandwich attacks on deposit/withdraw
rejected_candidates.reason: The claim is too generic for this share vault design and does not show a concrete profitable manipulation beyond the separate donation/inflation issue already captured.

rejected_candidates.title: No deadline parameter allows unfavorable transaction execution
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic UX/property complaint, not a concrete protocol vulnerability in the vault logic.

rejected_candidates.title: setController has no validation allowing malicious controller
rejected_candidates.reason: Choosing the controller is a governance trust boundary; governance can already point the vault at any controller, so this does not introduce an additional permissionless exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: earn() can be called by anyone triggering unnecessary operations
rejected_candidates.reason: Subsumed by the stronger finding that repeated public `earn()` calls can drain the local withdrawal buffer to dust.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing ERC20 transfer return value check in deposit (indirect)
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: `deposit()` uses `SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom`, which already checks call success and optional return values.
